framework.name: NIST Privacy Framework
framework.description: The NIST Privacy Framework is a voluntary tool to help organizations identify and manage privacy risk to build innovative products and services while protecting individuals' privacy. It consists of three parts: the Core (Functions, Categories, Subcategories), Profiles, and Implementation Tiers.
framework.verification_notes: All 5 Functions, 18 Categories, and 100 Subcategories verified against NIST Privacy Framework Version 1.0 (January 2020). Version 1.1 is currently in Initial Public Draft (April 2025); 1.0 remains the current stable standard.

concepts.definition_en: The five Functions of the Privacy Framework Core organize privacy activities at their highest level: Identify-P, Govern-P, Control-P, Communicate-P, and Protect-P.
concepts.definition_nb: The five Functions of the Privacy Framework Core organize privacy activities at their highest level: Identify-P, Govern-P, Control-P, Communicate-P, and Protect-P.

concepts.definition_en: Develop the organizational understanding to manage privacy risk for individuals arising from data processing.
concepts.definition_nb: Develop the organizational understanding to manage privacy risk for individuals arising from data processing.

concepts.definition_en: Develop and implement the organizational governance structure to enable an ongoing understanding of the organization's risk management priorities that are informed by privacy risk.
concepts.definition_nb: Develop and implement the organizational governance structure to enable an ongoing understanding of the organization's risk management priorities that are informed by privacy risk.

concepts.definition_en: Develop and implement appropriate activities to enable organizations or individuals to manage data with sufficient granularity to manage privacy risks.
concepts.definition_nb: Develop and implement appropriate activities to enable organizations or individuals to manage data with sufficient granularity to manage privacy risks.

concepts.definition_en: Develop and implement appropriate activities to enable organizations and individuals to have a reliable understanding and engage in a dialogue about how data are processed and associated privacy risks.
concepts.definition_nb: Develop and implement appropriate activities to enable organizations and individuals to have a reliable understanding and engage in a dialogue about how data are processed and associated privacy risks.

concepts.definition_en: Develop and implement appropriate data processing safeguards to prevent privacy incidents related to cybersecurity, highlighting the overlap of privacy and cybersecurity risk management.
concepts.definition_nb: Develop and implement appropriate data processing safeguards to prevent privacy incidents related to cybersecurity, highlighting the overlap of privacy and cybersecurity risk management.

concepts.definition_en: Data processing by systems, products, or services is understood and informs the management of privacy risk.
concepts.definition_nb: Data processing by systems, products, or services is understood and informs the management of privacy risk.

concepts.definition_en: Systems/products/services that process data are inventoried.
concepts.definition_nb: Systems/products/services that process data are inventoried.

concepts.definition_en: Organizations and third parties involved in data processing, along with their roles regarding systems and components, are documented.
concepts.definition_nb: Organizations and third parties involved in data processing, along with their roles regarding systems and components, are documented.

concepts.definition_en: Categories of individuals (e.g., customers, employees or prospective employees, consumers) whose data are being processed are inventoried.
concepts.definition_nb: Categories of individuals (e.g., customers, employees or prospective employees, consumers) whose data are being processed are inventoried.

concepts.definition_en: Data actions of the systems/products/services are inventoried.
concepts.definition_nb: Data actions of the systems/products/services are inventoried.

concepts.definition_en: The purposes for the data actions are inventoried.
concepts.definition_nb: The purposes for the data actions are inventoried.

concepts.definition_en: Data elements within the data actions are inventoried.
concepts.definition_nb: Data elements within the data actions are inventoried.

concepts.definition_en: The data processing environment is identified (e.g., geographic location, internal, cloud, third parties).
concepts.definition_nb: The data processing environment is identified (e.g., geographic location, internal, cloud, third parties).

concepts.definition_en: Data processing workflows are documented, showing data actions, associated elements, component ownership roles, and interactions with individuals or external parties.
concepts.definition_nb: Data processing workflows are documented, showing data actions, associated elements, component ownership roles, and interactions with individuals or external parties.

concepts.definition_en: The organization's mission, objectives, stakeholders, and activities are understood and used to inform privacy roles, responsibilities, and risk management decisions.
concepts.definition_nb: The organization's mission, objectives, stakeholders, and activities are understood and used to inform privacy roles, responsibilities, and risk management decisions.

concepts.definition_en: The organization's role(s) in the data processing ecosystem are identified and communicated.
concepts.definition_nb: The organization's role(s) in the data processing ecosystem are identified and communicated.

concepts.definition_en: Priorities for organizational mission, objectives, and activities are established and communicated.
concepts.definition_nb: Priorities for organizational mission, objectives, and activities are established and communicated.

concepts.definition_en: Systems/products/services that support organizational priorities are identified and key requirements communicated.
concepts.definition_nb: Systems/products/services that support organizational priorities are identified and key requirements communicated.

concepts.definition_en: The organization comprehends privacy risks to individuals and how such risks may create operational impacts including mission, functions, compliance, financial concerns, reputation, workforce, and culture.
concepts.definition_nb: The organization comprehends privacy risks to individuals and how such risks may create operational impacts including mission, functions, compliance, financial concerns, reputation, workforce, and culture.

concepts.definition_en: Contextual factors related to the systems/products/services and the data actions are identified (e.g., individuals' demographics and privacy interests or perceptions, data sensitivity and/or types, visibility of data processing to individuals and third parties).
concepts.definition_nb: Contextual factors related to the systems/products/services and the data actions are identified (e.g., individuals' demographics and privacy interests or perceptions, data sensitivity and/or types, visibility of data processing to individuals and third parties).

concepts.definition_en: Data analytic inputs and outputs are identified and evaluated for bias.
concepts.definition_nb: Data analytic inputs and outputs are identified and evaluated for bias.

concepts.definition_en: Potential problematic data actions and associated problems are identified.
concepts.definition_nb: Potential problematic data actions and associated problems are identified.

concepts.definition_en: Problematic data actions, likelihoods, and impacts are used to determine and prioritize risk.
concepts.definition_nb: Problematic data actions, likelihoods, and impacts are used to determine and prioritize risk.

concepts.definition_en: Risk responses are identified, prioritized, and implemented.
concepts.definition_nb: Risk responses are identified, prioritized, and implemented.

concepts.definition_en: The organization establishes priorities, constraints, risk tolerance, and assumptions to support risk decisions and has implemented processes to identify, assess, and manage privacy risks within the data processing ecosystem.
concepts.definition_nb: The organization establishes priorities, constraints, risk tolerance, and assumptions to support risk decisions and has implemented processes to identify, assess, and manage privacy risks within the data processing ecosystem.

concepts.definition_en: Data processing ecosystem risk management policies, processes, and procedures are identified, established, assessed, managed, and agreed to by organizational stakeholders.
concepts.definition_nb: Data processing ecosystem risk management policies, processes, and procedures are identified, established, assessed, managed, and agreed to by organizational stakeholders.

concepts.definition_en: Data processing ecosystem parties (e.g., service providers, customers, partners, product manufacturers, application developers) are identified, prioritized, and assessed using a privacy risk assessment process.
concepts.definition_nb: Data processing ecosystem parties (e.g., service providers, customers, partners, product manufacturers, application developers) are identified, prioritized, and assessed using a privacy risk assessment process.

concepts.definition_en: Contracts with data processing ecosystem parties are used to implement appropriate measures designed to meet the objectives of an organization's privacy program.
concepts.definition_nb: Contracts with data processing ecosystem parties are used to implement appropriate measures designed to meet the objectives of an organization's privacy program.

concepts.definition_en: Interoperability frameworks or similar multi-party approaches are used to manage data processing ecosystem privacy risks.
concepts.definition_nb: Interoperability frameworks or similar multi-party approaches are used to manage data processing ecosystem privacy risks.

concepts.definition_en: Data processing ecosystem parties are routinely assessed using audits, test results, or other forms of evaluations to confirm they are meeting their contractual, interoperability framework, or other obligations.
concepts.definition_nb: Data processing ecosystem parties are routinely assessed using audits, test results, or other forms of evaluations to confirm they are meeting their contractual, interoperability framework, or other obligations.

concepts.definition_en: The policies, processes, and procedures to manage and monitor the organization's regulatory, legal, risk, environmental, and operational requirements are understood and inform the management of privacy risk.
concepts.definition_nb: The policies, processes, and procedures to manage and monitor the organization's regulatory, legal, risk, environmental, and operational requirements are understood and inform the management of privacy risk.

concepts.definition_en: Organizational privacy values and policies (e.g., conditions on data processing such as data uses or retention periods, individuals' prerogatives with respect to data processing) are established and communicated.
concepts.definition_nb: Organizational privacy values and policies (e.g., conditions on data processing such as data uses or retention periods, individuals' prerogatives with respect to data processing) are established and communicated.

concepts.definition_en: Processes to instill organizational privacy values within system/product/service development and operations are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Processes to instill organizational privacy values within system/product/service development and operations are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: Roles and responsibilities for the workforce are established with respect to privacy.
concepts.definition_nb: Roles and responsibilities for the workforce are established with respect to privacy.

concepts.definition_en: Privacy roles and responsibilities are coordinated and aligned with third-party stakeholders (e.g., service providers, customers, partners).
concepts.definition_nb: Privacy roles and responsibilities are coordinated and aligned with third-party stakeholders (e.g., service providers, customers, partners).

concepts.definition_en: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding privacy are understood and managed.
concepts.definition_nb: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding privacy are understood and managed.

concepts.definition_en: Governance and risk management policies, processes, and procedures address privacy risks.
concepts.definition_nb: Governance and risk management policies, processes, and procedures address privacy risks.

concepts.definition_en: The organization's approach to managing privacy risk is established, understood, and communicated.
concepts.definition_nb: The organization's approach to managing privacy risk is established, understood, and communicated.

concepts.definition_en: Risk management processes are established, managed, and agreed to by organizational stakeholders.
concepts.definition_nb: Risk management processes are established, managed, and agreed to by organizational stakeholders.

concepts.definition_en: Organizational risk tolerance is determined and clearly expressed.
concepts.definition_nb: Organizational risk tolerance is determined and clearly expressed.

concepts.definition_en: The organization's determination of risk tolerance is informed by its role(s) in the data processing ecosystem.
concepts.definition_nb: The organization's determination of risk tolerance is informed by its role(s) in the data processing ecosystem.

concepts.definition_en: The organization is provided with awareness, education, and training to perform their privacy-related duties and responsibilities consistent with related policies, processes, and procedures.
concepts.definition_nb: The organization is provided with awareness, education, and training to perform their privacy-related duties and responsibilities consistent with related policies, processes, and procedures.

concepts.definition_en: The workforce is informed and trained on its roles and responsibilities.
concepts.definition_nb: The workforce is informed and trained on its roles and responsibilities.

concepts.definition_en: Senior executives understand their roles and responsibilities.
concepts.definition_nb: Senior executives understand their roles and responsibilities.

concepts.definition_en: Privacy personnel understand their roles and responsibilities.
concepts.definition_nb: Privacy personnel understand their roles and responsibilities.

concepts.definition_en: Third parties (e.g., service providers, customers, partners) understand their roles and responsibilities.
concepts.definition_nb: Third parties (e.g., service providers, customers, partners) understand their roles and responsibilities.

concepts.definition_en: The organization monitors its policies, processes, and procedures related to privacy and privacy-related activities are reviewed, assessed, and adjusted based on monitoring and evaluation activities.
concepts.definition_nb: The organization monitors its policies, processes, and procedures related to privacy and privacy-related activities are reviewed, assessed, and adjusted based on monitoring and evaluation activities.

concepts.definition_en: Privacy risk is re-evaluated on an ongoing basis and as key factors, including the organization's business environment (e.g., introduction of new technologies), governance (e.g., legal obligations, risk tolerance), data processing, and systems/products/services change.
concepts.definition_nb: Privacy risk is re-evaluated on an ongoing basis and as key factors, including the organization's business environment (e.g., introduction of new technologies), governance (e.g., legal obligations, risk tolerance), data processing, and systems/products/services change.

concepts.definition_en: Privacy values, policies, and training are reviewed and any updates are communicated.
concepts.definition_nb: Privacy values, policies, and training are reviewed and any updates are communicated.

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures for assessing compliance with legal requirements and privacy policies are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures for assessing compliance with legal requirements and privacy policies are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures for communicating progress on managing privacy risks are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures for communicating progress on managing privacy risks are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures are established and in place to receive, analyze, and respond to problematic data actions disclosed to the organization from internal and external sources.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures are established and in place to receive, analyze, and respond to problematic data actions disclosed to the organization from internal and external sources.

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures incorporate lessons learned from problematic data actions.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures incorporate lessons learned from problematic data actions.

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures for receiving, tracking, and responding to complaints, concerns, and questions from individuals about organizational privacy practices are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures for receiving, tracking, and responding to complaints, concerns, and questions from individuals about organizational privacy practices are established and in place.

concepts.name_en: Data Processing Policies, Processes, And Procedures
concepts.name_nb: Policyer, prosesser og prosedyrer for databehandling
concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures are established and in place to manage data processing in alignment with the organization's risk strategy.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures are established and in place to manage data processing in alignment with the organization's risk strategy.

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures for authorizing data processing (e.g., organizational decisions, individual consent), revoking authorizations, and maintaining authorizations are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures for authorizing data processing (e.g., organizational decisions, individual consent), revoking authorizations, and maintaining authorizations are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures for enabling data review, transfer, sharing or disclosure, alteration, and deletion are established and in place (e.g., to maintain data quality, manage data retention).
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures for enabling data review, transfer, sharing or disclosure, alteration, and deletion are established and in place (e.g., to maintain data quality, manage data retention).

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures for enabling individuals' data processing preferences and requests are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures for enabling individuals' data processing preferences and requests are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: A data life cycle to manage data is aligned and implemented with the system development life cycle to manage systems.
concepts.definition_nb: A data life cycle to manage data is aligned and implemented with the system development life cycle to manage systems.

concepts.definition_en: Data are managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to implement the data processing principles of transparency and accountability.
concepts.definition_nb: Data are managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to implement the data processing principles of transparency and accountability.

concepts.definition_en: Data elements can be accessed for review.
concepts.definition_nb: Data elements can be accessed for review.

concepts.definition_en: Data elements can be accessed for transmission or disclosure.
concepts.definition_nb: Data elements can be accessed for transmission or disclosure.

concepts.definition_en: Data elements can be accessed for alteration.
concepts.definition_nb: Data elements can be accessed for alteration.

concepts.definition_en: Data elements can be accessed for deletion.
concepts.definition_nb: Data elements can be accessed for deletion.

concepts.definition_en: Data are destroyed according to policy.
concepts.definition_nb: Data are destroyed according to policy.

concepts.definition_en: Data are transmitted using standardized formats.
concepts.definition_nb: Data are transmitted using standardized formats.

concepts.definition_en: Mechanisms for transmitting processing permissions and related data values with data elements are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Mechanisms for transmitting processing permissions and related data values with data elements are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed in accordance with policy and incorporating data minimization principles.
concepts.definition_nb: Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed in accordance with policy and incorporating data minimization principles.

concepts.definition_en: Technical measures implemented to manage data processing are tested and assessed.
concepts.definition_nb: Technical measures implemented to manage data processing are tested and assessed.

concepts.definition_en: Stakeholder privacy preferences are included in algorithmic design objectives and outputs are evaluated against these preferences.
concepts.definition_nb: Stakeholder privacy preferences are included in algorithmic design objectives and outputs are evaluated against these preferences.

concepts.definition_en: Data processing solutions are selected and implemented consistent with the organization's risk strategy to enable data to be processed while limiting the identification, linkability, and discoverability of individuals and their activities.
concepts.definition_nb: Data processing solutions are selected and implemented consistent with the organization's risk strategy to enable data to be processed while limiting the identification, linkability, and discoverability of individuals and their activities.

concepts.definition_en: Data are processed to limit observability and linkability (e.g., data actions take place on local devices, privacy-preserving cryptography).
concepts.definition_nb: Data are processed to limit observability and linkability (e.g., data actions take place on local devices, privacy-preserving cryptography).

concepts.definition_en: Data are processed to limit the identification of individuals (e.g., de-identification privacy techniques, tokenization).
concepts.definition_nb: Data are processed to limit the identification of individuals (e.g., de-identification privacy techniques, tokenization).

concepts.definition_en: Data are processed to limit the formulation of inferences about individuals' behavior or activities (e.g., data processing is decentralized, distributed architectures).
concepts.definition_nb: Data are processed to limit the formulation of inferences about individuals' behavior or activities (e.g., data processing is decentralized, distributed architectures).

concepts.definition_en: System or device configurations permit selective collection or disclosure of data elements.
concepts.definition_nb: System or device configurations permit selective collection or disclosure of data elements.

concepts.definition_en: Attribute references are substituted for attribute values.
concepts.definition_nb: Attribute references are substituted for attribute values.

concepts.definition_en: Policies, processes, and procedures are maintained and used to increase transparency of the organization's data processing practices and associated privacy risks.
concepts.definition_nb: Policies, processes, and procedures are maintained and used to increase transparency of the organization's data processing practices and associated privacy risks.

concepts.definition_en: Transparency policies, processes, and procedures for communicating data processing purposes, practices, and associated privacy risks are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Transparency policies, processes, and procedures for communicating data processing purposes, practices, and associated privacy risks are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: Roles and responsibilities (e.g., public relations) for communicating data processing purposes, practices, and associated privacy risks are established.
concepts.definition_nb: Roles and responsibilities (e.g., public relations) for communicating data processing purposes, practices, and associated privacy risks are established.

concepts.definition_en: Mechanisms and methods are provided to individuals to facilitate clear understanding of and engagement in dialogue about the purpose, scope, and impacts of data processing.
concepts.definition_nb: Mechanisms and methods are provided to individuals to facilitate clear understanding of and engagement in dialogue about the purpose, scope, and impacts of data processing.

concepts.definition_en: Mechanisms (e.g., notices, internal or public reports) for communicating data processing purposes, practices, and associated privacy risks are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Mechanisms (e.g., notices, internal or public reports) for communicating data processing purposes, practices, and associated privacy risks are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: Mechanisms for obtaining feedback from individuals (e.g., surveys or focus groups) about data processing and associated privacy risks are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Mechanisms for obtaining feedback from individuals (e.g., surveys or focus groups) about data processing and associated privacy risks are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: System/product/service design enables data processing visibility.
concepts.definition_nb: System/product/service design enables data processing visibility.

concepts.definition_en: Records of data disclosures and sharing are maintained and can be accessed for review or transmission/disclosure.
concepts.definition_nb: Records of data disclosures and sharing are maintained and can be accessed for review or transmission/disclosure.

concepts.definition_en: Data corrections or deletions can be communicated to individuals or organizations (e.g., data sources) in the data processing ecosystem.
concepts.definition_nb: Data corrections or deletions can be communicated to individuals or organizations (e.g., data sources) in the data processing ecosystem.

concepts.definition_en: Data provenance and lineage are maintained and can be accessed for review or transmission/disclosure.
concepts.definition_nb: Data provenance and lineage are maintained and can be accessed for review or transmission/disclosure.

concepts.definition_en: Impacted individuals and organizations are notified about a privacy breach or event.
concepts.definition_nb: Impacted individuals and organizations are notified about a privacy breach or event.

concepts.definition_en: Individuals are provided with mitigation mechanisms (e.g., credit monitoring, consent withdrawal, data alteration or deletion) to address impacts of problematic data actions.
concepts.definition_nb: Individuals are provided with mitigation mechanisms (e.g., credit monitoring, consent withdrawal, data alteration or deletion) to address impacts of problematic data actions.

concepts.name_en: Data Protection Policies, Processes, And Procedures
concepts.definition_en: Security and privacy policies, processes, and procedures are maintained and used to manage the protection of data.
concepts.definition_nb: Security and privacy policies, processes, and procedures are maintained and used to manage the protection of data.

concepts.definition_en: A baseline configuration of information technology is created and maintained incorporating security principles (e.g., concept of least functionality).
concepts.definition_nb: A baseline configuration of information technology is created and maintained incorporating security principles (e.g., concept of least functionality).

concepts.definition_en: Configuration change control processes are established and in place.
concepts.definition_nb: Configuration change control processes are established and in place.

concepts.definition_en: Backups of information are conducted, maintained, and tested.
concepts.definition_nb: Backups of information are conducted, maintained, and tested.

concepts.definition_en: Policy and regulations regarding the physical operating environment for organizational assets are met.
concepts.definition_nb: Policy and regulations regarding the physical operating environment for organizational assets are met.

concepts.definition_en: Protection processes are improved.
concepts.definition_nb: Protection processes are improved.

concepts.definition_en: Effectiveness of protection technologies is shared.
concepts.definition_nb: Effectiveness of protection technologies is shared.

concepts.definition_en: Response plans (Incident Response and Business Continuity) and recovery plans (Incident Recovery and Disaster Recovery) are established, in place, and managed.
concepts.definition_nb: Response plans (Incident Response and Business Continuity) and recovery plans (Incident Recovery and Disaster Recovery) are established, in place, and managed.

concepts.definition_en: Response and recovery plans are tested.
concepts.definition_nb: Response and recovery plans are tested.

concepts.definition_en: Privacy procedures are included in human resources practices (e.g., deprovisioning, personnel screening).
concepts.definition_nb: Privacy procedures are included in human resources practices (e.g., deprovisioning, personnel screening).

concepts.definition_en: A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented.
concepts.definition_nb: A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented.

concepts.name_en: Identity Management, Authentication, And Access Control
concepts.definition_en: Access to data and devices is limited to authorized individuals, processes, and devices, and is managed consistent with the assessed risk of unauthorized access.
concepts.definition_nb: Access to data and devices is limited to authorized individuals, processes, and devices, and is managed consistent with the assessed risk of unauthorized access.

concepts.definition_en: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited for authorized individuals, processes, and devices.
concepts.definition_nb: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited for authorized individuals, processes, and devices.

concepts.definition_en: Physical access to data and devices is managed.
concepts.definition_nb: Physical access to data and devices is managed.

concepts.definition_en: Remote access is managed.
concepts.definition_nb: Remote access is managed.

concepts.definition_en: Access permissions and authorizations are managed, incorporating the principles of least privilege and separation of duties.
concepts.definition_nb: Access permissions and authorizations are managed, incorporating the principles of least privilege and separation of duties.

concepts.definition_en: Network integrity is protected (e.g., network segregation, network segmentation).
concepts.definition_nb: Network integrity is protected (e.g., network segregation, network segmentation).

concepts.definition_en: Individuals and devices are proofed and bound to credentials, and authenticated commensurate with the risk of the transaction (e.g., individuals' security and privacy risks and other organizational risks).
concepts.definition_nb: Individuals and devices are proofed and bound to credentials, and authenticated commensurate with the risk of the transaction (e.g., individuals' security and privacy risks and other organizational risks).

concepts.definition_en: Data are managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to protect individuals' privacy and maintain data confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
concepts.definition_nb: Data are managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to protect individuals' privacy and maintain data confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

concepts.definition_en: Data-at-rest are protected.
concepts.definition_nb: Data-at-rest are protected.

concepts.definition_en: Data-in-transit are protected.
concepts.definition_nb: Data-in-transit are protected.

concepts.definition_en: Systems/products/services and associated data are formally managed throughout removal, transfers, and disposition.
concepts.definition_nb: Systems/products/services and associated data are formally managed throughout removal, transfers, and disposition.

concepts.definition_en: Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained.
concepts.definition_nb: Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained.

concepts.definition_en: Protections against data leaks are implemented.
concepts.definition_nb: Protections against data leaks are implemented.

concepts.definition_en: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify software, firmware, and information integrity.
concepts.definition_nb: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify software, firmware, and information integrity.

concepts.definition_en: The development and testing environment(s) are separate from the production environment.
concepts.definition_nb: The development and testing environment(s) are separate from the production environment.

concepts.definition_en: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify hardware integrity.
concepts.definition_nb: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify hardware integrity.

concepts.definition_en: System maintenance and repairs are performed consistent with policies, processes, and procedures.
concepts.definition_nb: System maintenance and repairs are performed consistent with policies, processes, and procedures.

concepts.definition_en: Maintenance and repair of organizational assets are performed and logged, with approved and controlled tools.
concepts.definition_nb: Maintenance and repair of organizational assets are performed and logged, with approved and controlled tools.

concepts.definition_en: Remote maintenance of organizational assets is approved, logged, and performed in a manner that prevents unauthorized access.
concepts.definition_nb: Remote maintenance of organizational assets is approved, logged, and performed in a manner that prevents unauthorized access.

concepts.definition_en: Technical security solutions are managed to ensure the security and resilience of systems/products/services.
concepts.definition_nb: Technical security solutions are managed to ensure the security and resilience of systems/products/services.

concepts.definition_en: Removable media is protected and its use restricted according to policy.
concepts.definition_nb: Removable media is protected and its use restricted according to policy.

concepts.definition_en: The principle of least functionality is incorporated by configuring systems to provide only essential capabilities.
concepts.definition_nb: The principle of least functionality is incorporated by configuring systems to provide only essential capabilities.

concepts.definition_en: Communications and control networks are protected.
concepts.definition_nb: Communications and control networks are protected.

concepts.definition_en: Mechanisms (e.g., failsafe, load balancing, hot swap) are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations.
concepts.definition_nb: Mechanisms (e.g., failsafe, load balancing, hot swap) are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations.
